header_summary:
Recent discussions on the Bitcoin Development Mailing List have addressed the execution of chain rollbacks in blockchain environments, revealing the complexities and potential repercussions of miners opting to mine older blockchain versions. Initiating a rollback by mining a "revisionist" chain poses substantial risks, including the potential betrayal of system integrity and significant financial consequences for the exchanges and miners involved. Preemptive measures like deploying an EC disabling fork are proposed to mitigate these risks and protect the network's stability. For more details, see the full discussion [here](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/Mh9z4ISxh4iA80hltDJ3_u7ZWSO5pFuMYI9ae0aGIoSXJDdTOi2KiI4Ckm9BfUAY7YEMvTofep7hyKsA7Qjk6ydqk2GqG9fvOzEym9YQKjc=@proton.me/T/#mfab3a03d0d786b69c0e40b0542e22d7ddba7b3d6).

Fabian's contributions to integrating Post-Quantum cryptographic schemes into Bitcoin Improvement Proposals explore various technical challenges and strategic considerations. The discussion spans the need for new witness versions, output types, and the potential impacts of Cryptographically Relevant Quantum Computers on network consensus and transactions. These insights are pivotal as the community prepares for future cryptographic shifts, with more details available on GitHub [here](https://github.com/fjahr/bips/pull/6discussion_r3609171527).

In mining operations, the introduction of proxies has complicated the management of variable difficulty (vardiff), necessitating a new control loop to handle aggregated data streams effectively. This structural adjustment aims to maintain fairness and efficiency in mining contributions across pools. The exploration of this topic, available [here](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/vardiff-belongs-at-the-frontier/2734), underscores the ongoing need to adapt infrastructure to maintain optimal network performance and miner equity.

recent_posts.title: The game-theory problems of PQ sunsetting modes
recent_posts.summary:
- Miners may abandon the authentic chain to mine a revisionist one.
- This action risks undermining system integrity and financial stability.
- Concerns about shallow chain reorganizations suggest preventive measures.

recent_posts.title: BIP draft: CISA for Taproot Key Path Spends
recent_posts.summary:
- Fabian discusses integrating Post-Quantum schemes into Bitcoin proposals, noting technical modifications needed.
- He evaluates trade-offs like public key visibility and data costs affecting security model benefits.
- Highlights strategic needs and potential impacts of Quantum Computers on Bitcoin network consensus.

recent_posts.title: Quantum Recovery Of Hashed Address Secured Coins With No Confiscatory Risk
recent_posts.summary: - Debate over Bitcoin rescue protocols post-Quantum Day discusses feasibility and scope.  - Concerns about the effectiveness of Knowledge Asymmetries for various address types are raised.  - Discussions challenge initial assumptions and explore ethical implications of xpub exposure.

recent_posts.title: [Research] A clockless vardiff strands a slowing miner
recent_posts.summary:
- A recent publication explores proxy integration in mining and its effects on vardiff management.
- Proxy use complicates direct performance monitoring of each miner by the pool server.
- Two controllers, for the pool and proxy, must synchronize to effectively manage share rates.

recent_posts.title: Vardiff belongs at the frontier
recent_posts.summary:
- Variable difficulty management in mining pools complicates with added intermediaries.
- Deployment of Stratum V2 allows efficient miner and pool performance tracking.
- Control loops placed at data alteration points ensure accurate difficulty adjustments.

recent_posts.title: Vulnerability Disclosure: Twin Memory Exhaustion DoS Vulnerabilities in Core Lightning
recent_posts.summary:
- Two critical DoS vulnerabilities discovered in Core Lightning's `connectd` and `gossipd` daemons.
- Fixes applied in releases v26.04 and v26.06rc2 after issues caused uncontrolled memory growth.
- Internship project's fuzzing detected vulnerabilities, leading to robust updates and system stability improvements.

active_posts.title: Quantum Recovery Of Hashed Address Secured Coins With No Confiscatory Risk
active_posts.summary:
- Quantum computing threatens cryptocurrency security; quantum-safe schemes are essential.
- Proposals include hierarchical proofs, stateful proofs, and commit-reveal schemes for recovery.
- Non-hashed address types remain vulnerable to quantum attacks despite proposed solutions.

active_posts.title: BIP draft: CISA for Taproot Key Path Spends
active_posts.summary:
- The proposed BIP introduces a new signature handling system with CISA.
- Details on half-aggregation and full-aggregation are in BIP 458 and BIP 459 respectively.
- Feedback from developers is crucial for refining the BIP, accessible for review online.

active_posts.title: The game-theory problems of PQ sunsetting modes
active_posts.summary: - The "tripwire" scenario utilizes a NUMS puzzle to potentially freeze legacy Bitcoin coins.  - Miners and quantum computing entities might collude, threatening $215 billion in P2Pk coins.  - Quantum advancements could compromise blockchain stability and necessitate new security measures.

active_posts.title: P2share: how to turn any network (or testnet!) into a bitcoin miner
active_posts.summary:
- The "p2share" network changes reward distribution, giving the entire bitcoin reward randomly to one shareholder.
- It maintains a compact coinbase output, minimizing transaction bloat and maximizing fee revenue.
- This model aims for fairness, efficiency, and trustlessness, potentially reshaping future blockchain and mining practices.

active_posts.title: A Bitcoin-native LLM: dataset, architecture and open questions
active_posts.summary:
- The proposed Bitcoin-native LLM will enhance interaction with Bitcoin-specific elements.
- It requires a comprehensive dataset from various Bitcoin-related sources for development.
- The architecture combines a fine-tuned base model and a tool-calling layer for live queries.

active_posts.title: Input-triggered transaction expiry
active_posts.summary:
- Input-triggered expiry uses `nSequence` for timelocks, enhancing transaction security.
- This method supports complex channel operations with fewer consensus changes needed.
- It strengthens blockchain efficiency and is resistant to common exploits.

today_in_history_posts.title: On the experiment of the Bitcoin Contracting Primitives WG and marking this community process "up for grabs"
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Last year's process was designed to improve the evaluation of Bitcoin covenant proposals.
- A working group focused on archiving primitives and protocols for advanced contracts.
- The author prioritized Lightning Network development over expanding covenant capabilities.

today_in_history_posts.title: Blinded Paths Doom Scenario
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Blinded paths in the Lightning Network enhance privacy but limit routing options.
- Use of compliant nodes may compromise decentralization and privacy through potential tracking.
- The author questions the trade-offs between privacy and potential risks in network routing.

today_in_history_posts.title: Bitcoin Version 2 only for conscious living individuals
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- The new Bitcoin fork restricts wallet creation using national identity numbers to prevent centralization.
- Institutional Bitcoins can be replicated into personal wallets with a set wealth limit.
- Alternatives to NIN, like biometrics, are considered for countries without reliable identification systems.
